questions.statement: In a arithmetic progression the 1<sup style="">st</sup> term is 7 and the third term is 28, what is the second term?
In an arithmetic progression, the first term is 17 and the 17<sup style="">th</sup> term is 97. Find the common difference.
The sum of 6 consecutive odd numbers is 144. What will be the product of first number and the last number?
How many numbers are there from 1 to 100 which are completely divisible by 7?
The sum of all integers between 81 and 719 which are divisible by 5 is
What is the average of the first 30 multiples of 8?
Find the sum of the first 151 terms of an AP whose first term and the third term are 175 and 185 respectively.
What is the sum of the first 14 terms of an A.P whose 10<sup>th</sup> and 6<sup>th</sup> terms are 7.25 and 3.25 respectively?
What is the first term of an A.P. series having total 25 terms whose last term is 314 and the common difference is 12?
Find the 52th term of the arithmetic progression 8, 17, 26, 35..&hellip;.&nbsp;
questions.explanation:
Given:

Arithmetic Progression: 8, 17, 26, 35...

First Term (a) = 8

Common Difference (d) = 17 - 8 = 9

Number of Terms (n) = 52

Formula used:

nth Term (Tn) = a + (n - 1) × d

Calculation:

T52 = 8 + (52 - 1) × 9

⇒ T52 = 8 + 51 × 9

⇒ T52 = 8 + 459

⇒ T52 = 467

∴ The correct answer is option (4).

questions.statement: <p>Two solutions of 97% and 90% purity are mixed resulting in 28 litres of mixture of 94% purity. How much is the quantity of the first solution in the resulting mixture?</p>

There are two containers of equal capacity. The ratio of milk to water in the first container is 3 : 1, in the second container 5 : 2. If they are mixed up, the ratio of milk to water in the mixture will be
In two mixture the ratio of alcohol and water is 2 : 7 and 5 : 4 respectively. In what ratio should these two mixture be mixed so that the ratio of alcohol to water in new mixture becomes 4 : 5?
The ratio of copper, zinc and nickel in German silver is 4 : 3 : 2. How many kilograms of zinc is added to this metal of 54 kg so that the new ratio becomes 2 : 5 : 1?
1 litre of water at 40&deg;C&nbsp; is mixed with 1 litre of water at 60&deg;C. What will be the approximate temperature of water after a certain time?
In a 50-liter solution of alcohol and water, which contains 40% alcohol, 10 liters of alcohol is added. Find the percentage of water in the final mixture.
Find the ratio in which a mixture of Rs 192 per kg is mixed with a mixture of Rs 150 per kg in such a way that when the final mixture is sold at Rs 194.40 per kg, it gives a profit of 20 percent?
In what ratio should wheat of Rs. 8.30 per kg be mixed with wheat of Rs. 9.80 per kg so that the value of the mixture obtained is Rs. 9 per kg?

In a mixture of 120 litres of alcohol and water, the ratio of alcohol to the water is 5&nbsp;∶ 7. If 30 litres of water is added to the mixture, then find the ratio of alcohol and water in the final mixture.
Two cups A and B contain juice and water in the ratio of 7:9 and 11:13, if these mixtures are mixed in the ratio of 2:3 then what is the ratio of&nbsp; juice and water in the new mixture ?
Find how much grams of copper be added to make the ratio as 4&nbsp;∶ 5, if zinc and copper are in the ratio of 5&nbsp;∶ 4 in 225 gm of an alloy.
A 12 litre solution of acid and water contains 30% acid. How much water (in litres) must be added to get a solution having 20% acid?
In what ratio must Suhail, a wholesaler, mix wheat worth ₹45 per kg with wheat worth ₹75 per kg, so that by selling the mixture at ₹80 per kg, he may gain 25%?
A shopkeeper has two types of rice, one costing ₹60 per kg and the other costing ₹80 per kg. He mixes them in the ratio of 3 : 2. What is the price per kg of the resulting mixture?
How many litres of milk with 24% concentration are to be mixed with 18 litres with 72% concentration to get a mixture with 36% concentration?
